title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)
communications.communication:
Dear Keweenaw County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Keweenaw County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)
communications.communication:
Hi Julie,

Thanks for your April 20, 2026 written response. As I understand your response to be a denial of my FOIA request — since the responsive records are being conditioned on a $1,250 fee not authorized under FOIA — please treat this message as a written appeal under MCL 15.240.

Three things to flag.

1. "Act or statute" in MCL 15.234(10) means a state statute that explicitly authorizes the sale or sets the fee. The Michigan Court of Appeals construed this carve-out in Grebner v. Clinton Charter Twp., 216 Mich. App. 736 (1996):

"A primary definition of the word 'specific' is 'explicit.' … In contrast, the Michigan Election Law, § 522, provides only for the payment of costs of preparing copies of voter registration records, as opposed to their sale. Clearly, this is not explicit authorization of the sale of voter registration rolls."

216 Mich. App. at 743.

MCL 48.101 governs transcripts from the tax roll maintained by the Treasurer's office. It does not explicitly authorize the sale of the Equalization Department's assessment roll, and it does not set a specific fee for such records. The $1,250 figure is a departmental/BoC fee-schedule number, not a fee "specifically provided by an act or statute" within the meaning of MCL 15.234(10).

EAPRA (MCL 15.441–15.446) fails the same test: it doesn't explicitly authorize the sale of assessment rolls, and it doesn't set a specific fee.

2. EAPRA has its own savings clause. MCL 15.443(4): "This act does not limit the right of a person to inspect or copy a public record under [FOIA]." The Senate Fiscal Agency's committee summary for the enacting bill (HB 5832, PA 462 of 1996) puts it plainly:

"The bill would not limit access to a public record under the FOIA. A public record available by enhanced access would have to be made available for inspection or copying in accordance with [FOIA]."

SFA Committee Summary HB 5832 (H-3), 9-18-96, at 2. (Attached.)

3. MI's FOIA has a clear list of exemptions under MCL 15.243. The records I requested are not listed there. FOIA (1976 PA 442) predates EAPRA (1996 PA 462) by ~20 years — if the Legislature had intended EAPRA or MCL 48.101 to make assessment records FOIA-exempt, they would have added them to 15.243.

Side point on custody. I am not requesting anything from the Treasurer's office — no tax billing records, no delinquent tax rolls, no payment receipts, no forfeiture records. I am requesting the assessment roll, which is maintained by the Equalization Department under MCL 211.34 / 211.34d. The assessment roll records valuations (SEV, taxable, classification, PRE). The Treasurer's tax roll records collection. Distinct records, distinct custodians — MCL 48.101 speaks to the Treasurer's records, not Equalization's. I also never requested GIS data.

Requested relief. Pursuant to MCL 15.240(2), I respectfully ask that you:

-- Reverse the denial; and
-- Produce the assessment roll at FOIA cost itemized under MCL 15.234 on the County's cost itemization form.
If this appeal is denied in whole or in part, I reserve all rights under MCL 15.240(1)(b), including a civil action in Keweenaw County Circuit Court with fee-shifting under MCL 15.240(6) and punitive damages under MCL 15.240(7) if the denial is found arbitrary and capricious.

Thanks,
Alexander Munro
awmunro@gmail.com · 734-272-6612
34 N Huron St, OFC
Ypsilanti, MI 48197

communications.communication:
Hi Julie,

Could you open a new request for me under the FOIA? This would be for a distinct category of records, for a distinct type of record from the tax rolls I was denied previously.

Could you please send me:
-Any correspondence (letters, emails) b/t Keweenaw County and Zillow (or Zillow, Inc; Zillow Group, etc), from the year 2010 thru current
-Any agreements (licenses, MOUs), invoices or contracts with Zillow or other data brokers who have purchased data sales like the $1250 fee quoted previously, from 2010 thru current
-Same as above (Correspondence, emails,q invoices & contracts) for ATTOM, Remine, Black Knight, First American  (aka Cotality/Corelogic) and any other data brokers who have purchased copies of Keweenaw property tax records at a cost above what's authorized under the foia, from 2010 thru current
-Same as above for BS&A software (the software suite which I believe the tax records are kept in after they come from the Board of Review), from 2010 thru current

If any of the above records do not exist, please confirm in writing per MCL15.235

I would appreciate a heads up if the cost for this new request exceeds $75 -- if it's less than that, I'll send a check.

Thank you,
Alexander Munro, 34 N Huron St OFC, Ypsilanti MI 48197

title: LAPD 1033/1208 Program Transfer Records (1996–1998)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: LAPD 1033/1208 Program Transfer Records (1996–1998)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I respectfully request copies of the following records maintained by the Defense Logistics Agency, DLA Disposition Services (formerly the Defense Reutilization and Marketing Service, DRMS/DRMO), and any predecessor offices that administered the 1208 Program and its successor 1033 Program:

1/ The complete application(s), Memorandum of Agreement, State Plan of Operation, and any signed participation agreements submitted by or on behalf of the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) to participate in the 1208 Program (10 U.S.C. § 2576, Pub. L. 101-189) and/or the 1033 Program (10 U.S.C. § 2576a, Pub. L. 104-201) between January 1, 1996 and December 31, 1998.
2/ All transfer records, requisition forms (including DD Form 1348-1A or equivalent), shipping documents, property accountability records, and inventory lists documenting the transfer of M16 rifles (NSN 1005-00-073-9421 or related national stock numbers) from any Department of Defense source to LAPD during that period — including but not limited to the approximately 600 M16 rifles reported to have been transferred to LAPD following the February 28, 1997 North Hollywood bank shootout.
3/ Records identifying the originating military installation(s) for the transferred rifles, including any documentation showing shipment from the Anniston Army Depot (Anniston, Alabama) or other Army depots.
4/ Any correspondence, memoranda, or email between DLA/DRMS/DRMO personnel and LAPD, the California State Coordinator for the 1033 Program, or the U.S. Army concerning the LAPD rifle transfer(s) during the above period.

Format: Electronic copies (PDF) via email are preferred.

Fee waiver: I request a fee waiver under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii) because disclosure is in the public interest, concerning the operations of government, specifically the militarization of civilian policing and the federal transfer of military weapons to local law enforcement. This request is not for commercial use. In the alternative, please notify me before incurring fees exceeding $25.

Expedited processing: Not requested.

Please segregate and release any non-exempt portions of responsive records and cite the specific exemption(s) relied upon for any withholding. If responsive records are maintained by another DoD component (e.g., U.S. Army TACOM, Anniston Army Depot, or the Army's Law Enforcement Support Office), please refer this request accordingly and notify me of the referral.

Sincerely,
Daniel Brown

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Dover School District/SAU11)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: Asian Giant Hornets (Washington State University)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Asian Giant Hornets (Washington State University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Widespread news reports and agency releases show that there has been the spread and distribution of "Asian Giant Hornets" / "Murder Hornets" in the last year. Earlier this year, a joint press conference was held about eradication efforts: https://agr.wa.gov/about-wsda/news-and-media-relations/news-releases?article=32279

Therefore, I request the following information:

- Any lab reports, internal communications, analyses, warnings, or other guidances about the Asian Giant Hornets.
- Any case reports about Asian Giant Hornet sightings or suspected sightings
 - Any sightings / emails / audio recordings / photos / videos from the public about Asian Giant Hornets sent through "agr.wa.gov/hornets, via email at hornets@agr.wa.gov, or by calling 1-800-443-6684" 
- Any forensic analysis or communication with law enforcement (state, local, or national) about Asian Giant Hornets.
- Any presentations or other documents prepared about Asian Giant Hornets
- Any photos or video related to Asian Giant Hornets

Please search from May 1, 2020 until the day this request is processed.

This request is being made for use on Motherboard, VICE Media's science and technology website. It is not for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Koebler

title: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Clara County Registrar of Voters)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Clara County Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in your County in that year. In case it is helpful to see an example of the type of data I am requesting, Alameda County has these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

Please let me know if you need any further clarification on the type of data that I am requesting.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

communications.communication:
Dear Aleksandra Conevska,


The Registrar of Voters’ Office has performed a diligent search in order to locate and produce the records that you requested, and we are providing a partial response today. The responsive records may be accessed here: [​Folder icon]  Aleksandra Conevska Nov 4 2024 CVRs<https://sccconnect-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/christina_rivaslouie_rov_sccgov_org/IgCs-Muy7ZFiR6kyY2gBZLqfAWSWRyvhgNO2sZaBwcW03TY?email=205375-13067991%40requests.muckrock.com&e=ldOhvA>. Please note, this link will expire in 30 days – download the files accordingly.  When prompted, each file may be viewed in the “Read Only” mode. We further note that certain information in the records you requested is exempt from disclosure and was redacted for reasons of personal privacy (Cal. Const. art. I, sec. I; Cal. Govt. Code §§ 7924.005, 7927.705, 7924.000; Cal. Evid. Code § 1040; City of San Jose v. Superior Court Santa Clara County (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 1008). We will be providing additional materials on a rolling basis going forward as the records are searched for, collected and appropriately examined for appropriate exemptions.  We anticipate providing you with the next batch of responsive records within one month from the date of this email.



Thank you.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (Santa Clara County Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
Hi,

Thank you for your prompt and helpful response to my request. I was able to download the read-only CVR files that correspond to the 2024 General Election. 

I understand you anticipate providing me with an additional batch of records and that this was only a partial response to my request, and I really appreciate you taking the time to locate and properly process the relevant records. I know you need more time but I'm hoping that in the meantime, you can clarify two things about the coming additional records you plan to try to provide to complete your response to my request. This will help for my own planning. 

First, could you clarify whether in the next batch of responsive records, you plan to try to provide them in a usable data format, rather than read-only? I understand you wanted to provide a quick partial response to my request and that the non-read only, usable redacted records, may take longer to process. But could you confirm whether you plan to try to provide the non-read only records where possible? I do not believe that read-only files comply with my request (at least for 2024 as the mandatory retention period has not yet expired) unless there is a technical reason why, and I'm happy to submit a technical note as to why that is. 

Second, could you clarify whether you are trying to locate the 2022 records, or if these have already been purged given the data retention requirements? I just want to know for my own planning if Santa Clara County no longer has the 2022 records at all, which I would understand as we are passed the mandatory record retention period. 

Apologies for the long message. I really appreciate your assistance on this, and all of the work your office does.

All my best,
Aleksandra

communications.communication:

If you could send the 2022 and 2024 CVR data in Excel format that would be perfect. When you say locked, however, do you mean it won't be accessible to work with? 

Thank you again and I look forward to hearing back. 

All best,
Aleksandra

title: GORA Request: Wireless CCTV

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: GORA Request: Wireless CCTV
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Wireless CCTV that were active any time between 1/1/2024 and 5/22/2026.
- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to Wireless CCTV dated between 1/1/2024 and 5/22/2026. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Positive drug screenings by facility

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Positive drug screenings by facility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A database or spreadsheet of the total number of positive drug screenings by facility at the time of this request and for the last five years. The West Virginia Division of Corrections and Rehabilitation Jail Units Inmate Handbook (https://wvpolicy.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/Jail-Handbook-All-Jails-2024.04.08.pdf) describes that each inmate is screened. I am not requesting the individual-level data, but aggregate data by facility. I request this data in a machine-readable database or spreadsheet format such as Excel, CSV, Access or SQL.

I also request all documentation that describes the layout of the database’s tables and fields, and that provides the definitions of any field names, codes, and abbreviations used in the database. This information may be gathered together in a single document like a data dictionary, entity layout, or user's guide. If that is the case, I request access to and copy of said document.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: RE: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Positive drug screenings by facility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following West Virginia Freedom of Information Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on May 12, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

title: FOIA Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Washington Secretary of State public records request #26-180
communications.communication:
Washington Secretary of State ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-180: Audrey: Thank you for your patience as we review your public records request with the Office of the Secretary of State. We will contact you on or before February 27, 2026 to provide either (i) any responsive records OSOS has located in that time or (ii) a revised estimate of when responsive records will be available or (iii) that there are no records responsive to your request. Please note that our estimate is based on multiple factors such as: (a) number of public records requests OSOS has open (b) the complexity of those requests (c) the complexity of the request you submitted (d) staff and other resources available to respond to public records requests. Additional dates may be needed to provide further batches of documents depending on the volume of the responsive records pertaining to your request. If you submitted a request that contains multiple parts and there are parts you wish OSOS to prioritize, please respond to this acknowledgment email so we are aware of what to prioritize for release. Please use the above Request ID# to see the status of your request by signing into the Washington Secretary of State portal.  Thank you, Public Records Office ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Washington Secretary of State.

communications.subject: Your Washington Secretary of State public records request #26-180 has been closed.
communications.communication:
Washington Secretary of State ************************************************************************ Record request #26-180 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: Hello – This is the last installment of records in response to your request. Your request has been fulfilled by the production of records (including any exemptions claimed) and is now closed. The Secretary of State’s Office does not intend to further address the request, and the Public Records Act's one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run as of the date of this message. Please contact us within 30 days by using your Request ID# on our portal if you have any questions. Responsive records are available to you on our portal. To preview or download your records, log into the portal and click the “All Requests” tab. You have two weeks to access your records from the portal. Please note that portions of several records in the previous batch were lawfully redacted according to RCW 5.60.060(2)(a); RCW 42.56.070(1) Attorney-Client Privilege. For additional records, please submit a new request. Have an excellent weekend! Regards, Public Records Office   ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Washington Secretary of State.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Catawba County Sheriff)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Catawba County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Department of War support for 2026 films "One Mile: Chapter One" and "One Mile: Chapter Two"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of War support for 2026 films "One Mile: Chapter One" and "One Mile: Chapter Two"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of War and the production of the 2026 films "One Mile: Chapter One" and "One Mile: Chapter Two," both directed by Adam Davidson. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; and all records of communications between employees of the Department of War and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "One Mile: Chapter One" and "One Mile: Chapter Two," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Public Information Request: Cowboy Concealments (Cypress-fairbanks Independent School District)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Public Information Request: Cowboy Concealments (Cypress-fairbanks Independent School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with Cowboy Concealments or Cowboy Streetlight Concealments that were active any time between 1/1/2024 and 5/21/2026.
- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to Cowboy Concealments or Cowboy Streetlight Concealments dated between 1/1/2024 and 5/21/2026. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Wirt County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Wirt County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kierstyn Aubrecht
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 20, 2026

19 Washington St, Elizabeth, WV 26143                                                                                              

To Whom it may concern,
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Wirt County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 

The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely,
Kierstyn Aubrecht
kra00019@mix.wvu.edu

database, please check back at a later date.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) concerning: 'Colonel Edward Philip Cutolo'.

Born : 04 Nov 1931 Manhattan, New York, USA
Died: 26 May 1980 Sculthorpe, Norfolk, United Kingdom

Branch: United States Army
Years of Service: 1954 to 1980

United States Military Academy Class of 1954
Cullum No. 20153

Please include the the following in addition to the OMPF:

1. Dates of service, dates of ranks, stations, list of awards
2. Current and previous assignment forms
3. Section II military education/training courses
4. Section V (Miscellaneous) form
5. Copies of commendations and award citations
6 . Any other releasable material such performance reports, discharge papers etc..

https://www.vlm.cem.va.gov/EDWARDPCUTOLO/75420
https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/183591851/edward-philip-cutolo
https://valor.militarytimes.com/recipient/recipient-145721/

PLEASE NOTE: I am requesting hard copies of the requested material.

Please scan and email PDF copies to: requests@muckrock.com

or mail hard copies to:

MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON MA 
02115-4577

In order to help you determine my status for the purpose of assessing fees, I am seeking information for historical research and not for any commercial benefit.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joseph Lloyd

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received president/chair of the board of alderman related to their elected position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on personal or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: RE: [External Email] New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)
communications.communication:
Please be advised that the City of Manchester is in receipt of your request for records below. The information you have requested is outside the control of this office and maintained by other departments to the extent that it exists.  We have requested our Information Systems Department to process your request as soon as possible.

The chairman of the Board of Mayor & Aldermen officially takes the position when appointed by their colleagues at the first organizational meeting of the board in January in even years, in this case January 6, 2026.  We will request chairman emails between 1/6/26 – 1/31/26 per the intent of your request.

A copy of your request and this communication has been sent to the aforementioned City representative.   You will be contacted when your request has been completed and arrangements can be made to view any related information.  I will provide you with an estimated time of completion as soon as I receive a response from the IT Department.

Matthew Normand
City Clerk
Office of the City Clerk
One City Hall Plaza
Manchester, NH 03101
T:(603) 792-6630 F:(603) 624-6481
www.manchesternh.gov/cityclerk<http://www.manchesternh.gov/cityclerk>

communications.subject: RE: [External Email] New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)
communications.communication:
Please be advised that your request for emails from the Manchester Board of Mayor & Aldermen chairman and should be completed by next Tuesday, March 31st.

You may make arrangements to view the emails in person with the City Clerk’s Office through cityclerk@manchesternh.gov<mailto:cityclerk@manchesternh.gov> , obtain an electronic copy of the emails, in person, on a thumb drive for $5.00 (cost of the thumb drive), or request that the thumb drive be mailed to you.  Once receive payment for the thumb drive and postage, if necessary, we will provide the documents requested upon completion.

Matthew Normand
City Clerk
Office of the City Clerk
One City Hall Plaza
Manchester, NH 03101

Matthew Normand

communications.subject: RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)
communications.communication: Good morning Mr. Normand, thanks so much for your time and help! If you can provide the responsive records as digital files as an upload or attached to a reply to this request, that would definitely be our preference. If not, we can pay for a USB and postage.

communications.subject: RE: [External Email] RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)
communications.communication:
Thank you for your response.  There are over three hundred responsive records to your request.  Unfortunately, the file is too large to attach to this email thread and I am unable to upload to your link.  If you send us payment in the amount of $5.74 for the USB and postage, I will be happy to mail it to you.

We accept cash, check made payable to the City of Manchester, or credit/debit payment on site.  We cannot accept credit/debit payment over the phone.

Sincerely,

Matthew Normand
City Clerk
Office of the City Clerk
One City Hall Plaza
Manchester, NH 03101
T:(603) 792-6630 F:(603) 624-6481
www.manchesternh.gov/cityclerk<http://www.manchesternh.gov/cityclerk>

communications.subject: RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Manchester City Clerk)
communications.communication:
No problem at all, that makes sense. Thank you again for your help. I can send a check made payable to the city. Should it be sent to: 

Office of the City Clerk
One City Hall Plaza
Manchester, NH 03101

Or a different mailing address?

title: Binford Road Inventory Forms

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Binford Road Inventory Forms
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All lists, records, notes, inventory forms, of all property seized on Binford Road, between January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

All pictures associated with all lists, records, inventory forms, of property seized on Binford Road  between January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

All inventory forms, pictures, records, made by Julia Barnes or her subordinates, pertaining to property seized on Binford Road  between January 1st, 2023-March 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

communications.subject: [External Message Added] County of Marin, CA public records request #26-308
communications.communication:
County of Marin, CA ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-308: Dear Requester, The County has reviewed your request and identified responsive records. The County will produce pictures/photographs of property seized from Binford Road that are not otherwise exempt from disclosure under the California Public Records Act (CPRA). The County requires additional time to collect and review these records. We anticipate producing the records on a rolling basis with an initial production on or before April 24, 2026.  The County is withholding certain responsive records pursuant to applicable exemptions under the CPRA. The applicable exemptions include: * * Records protected by the attorney-client privilege or attorney work product doctrine. Government Code Section 7927.705 and Evidence Code Section 950 et seq. * Records where the public interest in withholding the records clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure. Government Code Section 7922.000. * Personnel, medical or similar files, the disclosure of which would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. See Government Code Section 7927.700. * Records, the disclosure of which are exempted or prohibited pursuant to federal or state law. Government Code Section 7927.705. Thank you. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at County of Marin, CA.

title: Custer, Email Log - CPS

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Custer, Email Log - CPS
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. A log of all emails to/from the Jennifer Custer, from April 1, 2026 to April 30, 2026.

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment.

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2289329/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center. 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://cps.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=19586

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019586-051326
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
05/28/2026 Miriam Bhimani 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019586-051326 Thank you for using the Chicago Public Schools FOIA Center. You requested the following: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. A log of all emails to/from the Jennifer Custer, from April 1, 2026 to April 30, 2026. 

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2289329/ RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that certain private information was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. Student/family information has been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). Section 7.5(h) exempts from disclosure “information the disclosure of which is exempted under the State Officials and Employees Ethics Act, and records of any lawfully created State or local inspector general’s office that would be exempt if created or obtained by an Executive Inspector General’s office.” 5 ILCS 140/7.5(h). You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (McKinley County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (McKinley County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.communication:
We are in receipt of your request. We also received a previous request from you for a different date range. We will process the current request as part of the previous request. The request is burdensome and will require additional time to process. We do not have a database that contains the information you requested. We will search for documents responsive to your request. Each one of those documents will be reviewed and redacted in accordance with IPRA standards. We will review the number of responsive documents and determine if they can be delivered electronically. If not, the standard fee for a USB flash drive or CD-ROM would be $5.00.

Respectfully,

James Maiorano III
Sheriff, McKinley County

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records: 

1. A log of all emails to/from the Jennifer Biggs, from April 1, 2026 to April 30, 2026. 

Please search all CPS email accounts as well as personal accounts used for public business. The log should include but not be limited to: sender, recipient(s), date and time, subject line, and attachment. 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2289330/ RESPONSE: Documents responsive to your request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center at www.cps.edu/FOIARequests . Please log in with your username and password to access the records. Please note that certain private information was redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7. Specifically, section 7(1)(b) which exempts certain private information as defined by section 2(c-5) and section 7(1)(c) which exempts personal information contained within public records, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, unless the disclosure is consented to in writing by the individual subject of the information. “Unwarranted invasion of personal privacy” means the disclosure of information that is highly personal or objectionable to a reasonable person and in which the subject’s right to privacy outweighs any legitimate public interest in obtaining the information. Student/family information has been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7.5 (r) which exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois School Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA generally prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at is direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored.” (105 ILCS 10/2(d). You have a right to have the denial of your request reviewed by the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the Office of the Illinois Attorney General. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). You can file your Request for Review with the PAC by writing to: Leah Bartelt; Public Access Counselor Office of the Attorney General 500 South 2nd Street Springfield, Illinois 62706 E-mail: public.access@ilag.gov 877-299-3642 You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court. 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. Sincerely, Patricia Majchrowicz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Georgia Power
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act (O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq.), the City of Atlanta Department of Procurement acknowledges receipt of your request submitted via email on Wednesday, May 27, 2026 at 8:52 AM. Please accept this as the Department’s statutorily required response.


In your request of May 27, 2026, you asked for the following:


A copy of purchase order number 52524873, dated 6/26/2025 from Georgia Power for $6,120.- Attached

A copy of all agreements with Georgia Power related to purchase order number 52524873.- Please be advised that DOP has no responsive agreement or contract document related to your request for Georgia Power. The procurement was processed as a small purchase in accordance with the City of Atlanta Procurement Code. Under the City’s procurement regulations, a small purchase is a procurement made for goods or services that falls within the monetary threshold established for streamlined purchasing procedures and does not require a formal written contract or competitive sealed solicitation process. As such, no formal agreement was executed for this purchase.

Accordingly, there are no additional responsive documents in DOP’s custody and control related to a contract or agreement for this procurement.

Please let us know if you have any additional questions.

title: FBI Pensacola Resident Agency (Jacksonville Field Office) Statistics, Procurement, and Task Force Administration

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI Pensacola Resident Agency (Jacksonville Field Office) Statistics, Procurement, and Task Force Administration
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

FOIA Request: Pensacola Resident Agency (Jacksonville Field Office) Statistics, Procurement, and Task Force Administration

To the FBI FOIA/PA Division:

Under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552), I am requesting access to specific administrative, statistical, and procurement records pertaining to the operations of the Pensacola Resident Agency (RA), which operates under the jurisdiction of the Jacksonville Field Office, focusing on activities within Escambia County, Florida.

To avoid undue burden and ensure a precise search, this request does not seek raw intelligence, personally identifiable information (PII), or active investigative files. Instead, it seeks the following high-level administrative and statistical records generated between October 1, 2022, and the present date:

1. Statistical Accomplishment and Case Metrics
A statistical breakdown or summary report of the number of investigative cases opened, closed, or currently active at the Pensacola RA, categorized by FBI Classification Code or programmatic area. Specifically, I am requesting the raw case count numbers for operations falling under:

Counterintelligence (CI)

Counterterrorism (CT)

Cyber Crime

Violent Crime / Safe Streets

Public Corruption

2. Watchlisting and Task Force Administration

A copy of all active Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), Joint Task Force agreements, or operational charters between the FBI (Jacksonville FO / Pensacola RA) and the Escambia County Sheriff’s Office (ECSO) and/or the Pensacola Police Department (PPD).

Any localized administrative guidelines, memorandums, or statistical reports regarding the management, nomination, or auditing of the localized Terrorist Screening Database (TSDB) / "threat watchlisting" protocols in conjunction with Escambia County local law enforcement partners.

3. Procurement, Inventory, and Budgetary Allocation

Copies of localized procurement logs, purchase orders, or budgetary allocations specifically designated for the Pensacola RA.

Vendor contracts or equipment leases originating from or executed for the Pensacola RA, specifically pertaining to IT administration, network infrastructure, office space leases, and surveillance/telecommunications hardware.

Fee Waiver Request:
I request a waiver of all fees for this request. Disclosure of the requested information to me is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government—specifically, how federal law enforcement resources, budget allocations, and inter-agency task forces are being utilized in the Escambia County region. This request is not being made for commercial purposes.

If any portion of this request is denied or heavily redacted, please provide a detailed justification citing the specific FOIA exemptions applied. If the search for any specific item is deemed too broad, please process the remaining items and contact me to refine the scope of the delayed item.

Thank you for your lawful compliance and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

Under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552), I am requesting access to specific administrative, statistical, and procurement records pertaining to the operations of the Pensacola Resident Agency (RA), which operates under the jurisdiction of the Jacksonville Field Office, focusing on activities within Escambia County, Florida.

To avoid undue burden and ensure a precise search, this request does not seek raw intelligence, personally identifiable information (PII), or active investigative files. Instead, it seeks the following high-level administrative and statistical records generated between October 1, 2022, and the present date:

1. Statistical Accomplishment and Case Metrics
A statistical breakdown or summary report of the number of investigative cases opened, closed, or currently active at the Pensacola RA, categorized by FBI Classification Code or programmatic area. Specifically, I am requesting the raw case count numbers for operations falling under:

Counterintelligence (CI)

Counterterrorism (CT)

Cyber Crime

Violent Crime / Safe Streets

Public Corruption

2. Watchlisting and Task Force Administration

A copy of all active Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), Joint Task Force agreements, or operational charters between the FBI (Jacksonville FO / Pensacola RA) and the Escambia County Sheriff’s Office (ECSO) and/or the Pensacola Police Department (PPD).

Any localized administrative guidelines, memorandums, or statistical reports regarding the management, nomination, or auditing of the localized Terrorist Screening Database (TSDB) / "threat watchlisting" protocols in conjunction with Escambia County local law enforcement partners.

3. Procurement, Inventory, and Budgetary Allocation

Copies of localized procurement logs, purchase orders, or budgetary allocations specifically designated for the Pensacola RA.

Vendor contracts or equipment leases originating from or executed for the Pensacola RA, specifically pertaining to IT administration, network infrastructure, office space leases, and surveillance/telecommunications hardware.

Fee Waiver Request:
I request a waiver of all fees for this request. Disclosure of the requested information to me is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government—specifically, how federal law enforcement resources, budget allocations, and inter-agency task forces are being utilized in the Escambia County region. This request is not being made for commercial purposes.

If any portion of this request is denied or heavily redacted, please provide a detailed justification citing the specific FOIA exemptions applied. If the search for any specific item is deemed too broad, please process the remaining items and contact me to refine the scope of the delayed item.

Thank you for your lawful compliance and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht--

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1732075-000
communications.communication:
To the Public Information Officer:

I am in receipt of your letter dated April 17, 2026, regarding FOIPA Request No. 1732483-000 (Statistical Accomplishment and Case Metrics for the Pensacola RA), which denied my request for a public interest fee waiver.

In order to prevent this request from being administratively closed and to ensure processing continues immediately, I hereby agree to pay standard processing fees up to $25.00.

I reserve my right to appeal any final fee assessment. Furthermore, as I have been categorized as a "general (all others) requester," I respectfully remind the Bureau that under standard FOIA fee regulations [5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iv)(II)], I am entitled to the first two (2) hours of search time and the first one hundred (100) pages of duplication completely free of charge before any billable fees are incurred.

Given that statistical summaries are generally brief, I expect the responsive records to fall well within this free threshold.

Please consider this my formal authorization to proceed. I look forward to the release of these records.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1732075-000
communications.communication:
To the FBI FOIA/PA Division / Information Management Division:

RE: Administrative Fee Stipulations for FOIPA Request Nos. 1732075-000 and 1732483-000

I am in receipt of your correspondence dated April 17, 2026 (regarding Request No. 1732483-000) and April 28, 2026 (regarding Request No. 1732075-000). To ensure the administrative files for both tracking numbers are complete and processing is not delayed, please note the following directives for the official record:

Regarding FOIPA Request No. 1732483-000 (Statistical & Case Metrics):
While I disagree with the assessment, I will not appeal the public interest fee waiver denial at this time in order to expedite processing. As an "All Other" requester, I hereby invoke my statutory entitlement to two (2) free hours of search time and 100 pages of free duplication. Please proceed with the search using this standard allowance.

Regarding FOIPA Request No. 1732075-000 (Inter-Agency MOUs):
I acknowledge the "unusual circumstances" extension applied to this file. I am reiterating my request for a public interest fee waiver for this specific tracking number, as the disclosure of executed inter-agency agreements significantly contributes to the public's understanding of joint federal/local law enforcement operations.

Global Fee Cap Stipulation (Applicable to Both Requests):
For both of the above-referenced requests, if the processing costs exhaust the statutory free search time and duplication allowances, I stipulate a hard maximum fee cap of $25.00 per request.

Do not exceed $25.00 on either file. If the estimated fees to process either request exceed this cap, you are instructed to pause processing on that specific file and provide me with a detailed, itemized fee estimate so that I may narrow the scope accordingly.

Please update both administrative files and proceed with the searches.

title: PD Settlement Agreements

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: PD Settlement Agreements
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I am resubmitting this request originally forwarded to the city by the Elizabeth Reilly at the Nashua Police Department in January. Would you be able to assist with these records? Please let me know, and thank you for your help. 

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

 A copy of every agreement to settle a lawsuit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim against the police department over the past 10 years. This covers all agreements that would fall under New Hampshire RSA 91-A:4 (Right-to-Know Law), VI:

VI. Every agreement to settle a lawsuit against a governmental unit, threatened lawsuit, or other claim, entered into by any political subdivision or its insurer, shall be kept on file at the municipal clerk's office and made available for public inspection for a period of no less than 10 years from the date of settlement.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen
